ex.en: There was a three-story building on the corner.

enOverride: a sandwich made of a cooked patty of ground meat, usually served in a round bun

ex.en: He ordered a cheese burger for lunch.
She takes the bus to work every day.
He bought his brother's business.
She is busy with her work.
I like tea, but I don't like coffee.
ex.ko: 나는 차를 좋아하지만 커피는 좋아하지 않는다.

ex.en: She spread butter on the toast.

enOverride: an informal way of saying goodbye

ex.en: Bye! See you tomorrow.

She baked a birthday cake.
She reported several anonymous calls.
ex.ko: 그녀는 익명의 전화 여러 통을 신고했다.

ex.en: He took a photo with his camera.
We stayed at a camp near the lake.

enOverride: used to say that someone is able to do something, or that something is allowed

ex.en: I can swim very well.
ex.ko: 나는 수영을 아주 잘할 수 있다.

ex.en: The kids love candy.

The cat slept on the sofa all day.
Try to catch the ball!

enOverride: a compact disc used to store music or data

ex.en: She bought a new CD.
He listened to music on his CD player.

ex.en: The change was intended to increase sales.

enOverride: an informal word for a police officer

enOverride: used as the past form of 'can', or to make a polite request

enOverride: the past tense form of the verb 'do'

enOverride: used to perform an action, or as a helping verb in questions and negatives

ex.ko: 몸이 너무 안 좋아서 병원에 갔다.

enOverride: the third-person singular present form of the verb 'do'

ex.en: She does her homework every day.
